ISM manufacturing PMI | Intraday post (06/Jan)

Weekly post:

Challenges for 2026 | Weekly post (05 - 09/Jan)

I dropped a big post about the yearly outlook of challenges and market/economy risks:

The challenges of 2026

In the post that follows, I have compiled the geopolitical, economic, and equity-market developments and challenges that have embedded significant—yet still underpriced—risk in the market.

Spend some time with it!

ISM Manufacturing PMI for December came out as a miss. tariffs are cited as raising costs and weighing on demand while price pressures persist. Market needs tomorrow PMI and NFP to valuate the tendency.

Hidden vomma risk is visible:

Market is getting extremely vulnerable.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

The net speed profile is heavily negative, especially to the upside, with upside vol of vol being heavily sold. The most sticky zone is the centroid area of 6905.28 - 6910.82The primal bet is that the market rangebounds around it.

Upside pivot is at 6926.24with target of 6936.46Market prices a reversion opportunity here, and the chance is low to break above it. If some very good news would happen and market would oscillate a lot around the pivot with contant call skew bid and low RV, it can make it to 6950 and pin there, but thats right-tail, low probability scenario. Expected range is compressed.

Downside pivot is at 6878.87with downside target of 6858.23There is a bit more room to move into this direction, with downside target being magnetic if pivot held as resistance until the PM session.Margin of error is 6820/30 if vol would catch up, but we can expect reversion from this point.

Overall this is rather a long theta day for me. Ahead of PMI and NFP.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
